content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To         :                                                Date    :    1/22/76


From  :  FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
         Ken Finney

Subject:  LEGAL FEES FOR THE 2/3 TAX INITIATIVE ACTION

         In a phone conversation with Vince Thorpe, counsel
for the 2/3 Tax Initiative Committee, he indicated that
the committee is low on funds and that the legal fees
for the action against the FPPC may be paid by certain
individuals. He wondered whether this activity will
create any reporting obligations. I indicated that the
payments might be construed as an in-kind contribution
to the committee and reportable as such. I also indicated
that an expenditure of this nature, by the committee,
probably would be reportable.

         On the other hand, I suggested that if he has any
questions about the foregoing, he should submit an
opinion request to the Commission. I cautioned him
that I merely was providing staff advice and not a
definitive interpretation.


KF:pvp

- 2 -

their activity supporting petition circulation drives. I will send
you a copy of the draft as soon as it is available.

With regard to a proponent's filing obligation, our staff recommends that in a situation, such as you describe, where an individual or group acts under the direction or supervision of the proponent, the individual or group incurs the proponent's filing obligation. Independent groups or individuals which assist the campaign to qualify the initiative for the ballot do not have an obligation to file separate campaign statements; rather, the proponent's statement should include information relative to contributions and expenditures of all groups or individuals who acted in support of the drive.

The Commission does not have a mailing list to which it routinely sends copies of opinions as they are adopted. We do, however, publish periodically the FPPC Bulletin which summarizes opinions and regulations. Those of interest to readers are then available upon request. There are services available to which one may subscribe to receive opinions and regulations on a regular basis. These services are described in an article in the enclosed FPPC Bulletin.

I hope this information is helpful. Please contact our staff if you have further questions.

Sincerely,

Nan Hambleton
Special Compliance Representative

NH:bw

Enclosure

bcc: Jeanne P.

sections.question_synthetic: Whether legal fees paid by individuals to a political committee's action against the FPPC create reporting obligations under campaign finance law.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Staff indicated that such payments might constitute in-kind contributions requiring reporting by the committee, and that any expenditures of this nature by the committee would likely be reportable, but recommended that the committee submit a formal opinion request for a definitive interpretation.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Document is an internal memorandum rather than a formal advice letter response. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present. Staff explicitly cautioned that the advice given was not a definitive interpretation. Bottom portion of document is partially illegible due to OCR quality issues.

embedding.summary: This is an internal FPPC memorandum documenting a phone conversation between FPPC staff member Ken Finney and counsel for the 2/3 Tax Initiative Committee regarding potential reporting obligations for legal fees related to litigation against the FPPC.
